frameworks.name: prefrontal theories of conscious perception
frameworks.one_line: PFC activity is essential for subjective conscious experience

frameworks.name: no-report paradigms / posterior theories
frameworks.one_line: view that PFC involvement reflects task reports, not awareness

core_question: Do null findings from low-sensitivity studies refute prefrontal cortex involvement in conscious perception?
key_assumptions: subjective experience can be dissociated from objective task performance
method sensitivity determines whether PFC effects are detectable
manipulations: review of PFC lesion outcomes on subjective perception
review of PFC activity decoding for perceptual content
review of report vs no-report paradigms
key_findings: PFC lesions do affect subjective perception
PFC activity reflects specific perceptual content
PFC involvement is not solely driven by report requirements
interpretations: PFC plays an essential role in enabling subjective perceptual experience
conflating objective task capacity with subjective experience causes confusion
limitations_noted: claims depend on excluding low-sensitivity methods from the evidence base
